sections.paragraph:
ἐπιτρέπω 1 aor. ἐπέτρεψα. Pass.: fut. inf. ἐπιτραπήσεσθαι (Just., A I, 47, 5); 2 aor. pass. ἐπετράπην; pf. ἐπιτέτραμμαι (τρέπω ‘turn/direct toward’; Hom.+).

① to allow someone to do someth., allow, permit (so Pind. et al.) τινί someone w. inf. foll. (X., An. 1, 2, 19, Hell. 6, 3, 9; Epict. 1, 10, 10; 2, 7, 12; PMagd 2, 7 [221 b.c.]; 12, 11; PRyl 120, 16; Job 32:14; 4 Macc 4:18; ParJer 1:4; Jos., Ant. 8, 202; Just., Ath.) Mt 8:21; 19:8; Mk 10:4; Lk 8:32a; 9:59, 61; Ac 21:39; 27:3; 1 Ti 2:12; Dg 10:2; IEph 10:1; IRo 6:3; Hm 4, 1, 4; D 10:7; Ox 840, 12. Without inf. (Pind., O. 3, 36; SIG 260, 16; 360, 15; 34; Just., D. 141, 2; Tat. 20, 1; Ath. 1, 2; 2, 4): ἐπέτρεψεν αὐτοῖς he gave them permission Mk 5:13; Lk 8:32b. ᾧ ἂν αὐτὸς ἐπιτρέψῃ whomever he permits = appoints ISm 8:1. οὐκ ἐμαυτῷ ἐπιτρέψας not on my own initiative Pol 3:1. Abs. (Pind., O. 6, 21; X., Cyr. 5, 5, 9; SIG 490, 10; 591, 36) ἐπέτρεψεν ὁ Πιλᾶτος P. gave his permission J 19:38; cp. Ac 21:40; MPol 7:2. Of God (BGU 451, 10 [I/II a.d.] θεῶν ἐπιτρεπόντων; 248, 15 [cp. Dssm., NB 80=BS 252]; Jos., Ant. 20, 267 κἂν τὸ θεῖον ἐπιτρέπῃ) 1 Cor 16:7; Hb 6:3.—Pass. (SIG 1073, 44; 52; POxy 474, 40) ἐπιτρέπεταί τινι someone is permitted w. inf. foll. (Jos., Ant. 5, 3) Ac 26:1; 28:16; 1 Cor 14:34. Abs. ἐκείνῳ ἐπιτέτραπται it is entrusted to him = it is his duty Hv 2, 4, 3.

② order, instruct (X., An. 6, 5, 11; Arrian, Ind. 23, 5 ἐ. τινί; Cass. Dio, Fgm. 40, 5 Boiss. Cod.; PLond 1173, 3; Jos., Vi. 138) w. dat. of pers. and inf. 1 Cl 1:3.—B. 1340. Freq. as v.l. for ἐπιστρέφω.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

foll. foll. = followed, following

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Hv Hv = Visions

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 384
